commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `runEmbeddedAttempt` to assign materialized bundled MCP and LSP runtimes to the existing cleanup-scoped variables and adds cleanup-helper tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +40. Total +40 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main declares outer cleanup handles and then shadows them with inner `const` runtime bindings before cleanup passes the outer values. I did not run a live filesystem MCP sidecar repro in this read-only review.

target.title: fix: prevent bundleMcpRuntime/bundleLspRuntime variable shadowing in embedded attempt
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87482 — MCP filesystem sidecar duplicate spawn / process leak caused by variable shadowing.

## Root Cause

In `runEmbeddedAttempt()` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`):

- **Line 846**: `let bundleMcpRuntime` declared at function scope for cleanup tracking
- **Line 1364**: `const bundleMcpRuntime` declared in a nested block scope, **shadowing** the outer `let`
- **Line 4747**: cleanup references the outer `let`, which is still `undefined`

Same pattern for `bundleLspRuntime` (lines 847, 1378, 4748).

When cleanup ran `cleanupEmbeddedAttemptResources()`, it passed `undefined` for both runtimes, so `dispose()` was never called. This meant:
1. MCP leases were never released (`activeLeases > 0` persisted)
2. The session runtime manager skipped idle eviction
3. Stdio MCP child processes accumulated indefinitely

## Fix

Removed `const` from the inner declarations (lines 1364 and 1378) so they assign to the outer `let` variables instead of creating shadowing block-scoped bindings.

**Files changed:**
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — 2 lines changed
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts` — 2 regression tests added

## Tests

- Added test verifying `bundleMcpRuntime` and `bundleLspRuntime` are properly disposed during cleanup
- Added test verifying graceful handling when both are `undefined`
- All 12 existing + new tests pass
target.author: hoobnn
